title: LoL Esports |
content:
Before homeguards and turret plates, there was one of the most iconic rivalries in North America: TSM vs. CLG. On July 24, we’re running this beloved matchup back in League Classic.

As part of the League Classic Takeover of the LCS Summer Split Opening Week Presented by Mastercard, we’re welcoming back some of our most iconic players (and you!) for a special showmatch featuring League of Legends’ latest release. Join us at LCS CLASSIC and re-experience the NALCS on the original Summoner’s Rift. For one day only, it’s time to pick a side again.

LCS CLASSIC: TSM vs. CLG takes place on Friday, July 24, live from the Riot Games Arena in Los Angeles.

Tickets for LCS CLASSIC go on sale at10:00 am PDT on July 15via Tixr. This is a specialty ticketed event, so make sure to secure your seat early!

Wondering which familiar faces will return to the Rift? Keep an eye on the LCSOfficial X account for roster reveals!

title: MSI 2026 Grand Final falls short of T1 vs G2 viewership peak
summary: MSI 2026 Grand Final falls short of T1 vs G2 viewership peak  Esports Charts
content:
LIVE Viewership Updates & Audience Trends

Hanwha Life EsportsdefeatedBilibili Gaming(3:2) to win the2026 Mid-Season Invitational, claiming the organization’s first MSI title in its debut appearance at the tournament. The victory also kept the trophy in South Korea for a third consecutive season, following Gen.G’s back-to-back championships in 2024 and 2025.

The grand final reached 2.27 million Peak Viewers (excluding Chinese livestreaming platforms), falling short of the 2.32 million recorded duringT1’s match againstG2 Esportsearlier in the bracket. This made MSI 2026 the first edition since 2023 in which the grand final was not the tournament’s most-watched match.

MSI 2026 recorded a noticeable year-over-year decline compared with the record-setting 2025 edition. Total Hours Watched fell by more than 17%, while Peak Viewers dropped by over 32%. Part of that decline can be explained by the tournament’s shorter airtime, with this year’s event running roughly eight hours less than in 2025.

T1’s relatively early elimination was another major factor: the team remains the biggest viewership driver not only at MSI, but across the wider League of Legends esports ecosystem, and interest naturally weakened once it exited the competition.

Average Viewers proved considerably more resilient, declining by just 7.8% year over year. This suggests that, despite the loss of its strongest audience draw and a shorter overall broadcast schedule, MSI 2026 still maintained a solid underlying level of interest throughout the event. The downturn was therefore meaningful, but less severe on a per-minute basis than the headline Hours Watched and peak figures might suggest.

title: G2 Esports bench Dylan Falco, promote Perkz to Head Coach
summary: G2 Esports bench Dylan Falco, promote Perkz to Head Coach  sheepesports.com
content:
Your premier source for esports news, exclusive leaks, and expert analysis. Stay ahead of the game with the latest updates from League of Legends, Valorant, CS2, and Rocket League.

A few hours after G2 Esports' elimination from MSI, G2 Esports announced it would bench head coach Dylan Falco and replace him with Perkz. The reasons behind the decision are detailed below.

"Why does everyone seem to think they know how good a coach is, including a bunch of circle jerking coaches that think just because they have been doing it for years that an ex pro player with no coaching experience cant come in and be 1000x more influencial to a teams success idgi"

In 2019, I first appeared on Twitter as LEC Wooloo, sharing random roster rumors. Over time, I learned to become reliabl...

title: Hanwha Life Esports Crowned MSI Champions After Beating Bilibili Gaming In The Final
summary: Hanwha Life Esports Crowned MSI Champions After Beating Bilibili Gaming In The Final  Hotspawn
content:
Hanwha Life Esportshave ensured that the Mid-Season Invitational 2026 trophy will remain in South Korea, defeatingBilibili Gamingin a thrilling five-game grand final. The victory also brings an end to BLG’s Golden Road aspirations. Despite losing just a single best-of-five throughout the tournament, it came in the one that mattered most, the MSI Grand Final.

🏆@HLEOFFICIALARE THE#MSI2026CHAMPIONS 🏆pic.twitter.com/p0lbEc1Y1r

— LoL Esports (@lolesports)July 12, 2026

Both teams enjoyed the strongest runs of the tournament. BLG never dropped to the lower bracket, defeating T1, LYON, and HLE in the Upper Bracket Final. On the other side, Hanwha Life began their campaign by sweeping Team Secret Whales andG2 Esportsbefore falling to BLG in the Upper Bracket Final. They then eliminated LYON in the Lower Bracket Final to earn a rematch in the grand final, where they got their revenge.

Today’s best-of-five was anything but one-sided. The two teams battled for hours in front of an enthralled Korean crowd, and the deciding game perfectly encapsulated the series: constant teamfights and a gold graph that swung back and forth from one side to the other. HLE and BLG came into the tournament as the two favorites and the teams widely regarded as the best in the world, and they more than lived up to the hype.

HLE ultimately came out on top in this showdown. Lee “Gumayusi” Min-hyung delivered yet another incredibly clutch performance, continuing his habit of rising to the occasion on the biggest international stage. Meanwhile, Choi “Zeus” Woo-je and Kim “Zeka” Geon-woo made history by becoming the first players to win all three of Riot Games’ international League of Legends tournaments, further cementing their place among the game’s all-time greats.

title: Hanwha Life Esports Win League of Legends MSI 2026 Final 3-2 Over Bilibili Gaming
summary: Hanwha Life Esports Win League of Legends MSI 2026 Final 3-2 Over Bilibili Gaming  gHacks
content:
Hanwha Life Esports claimed the title at the League of Legends Mid-Season Invitational 2026 on July 12, defeating Bilibili Gaming 3-2 in the Grand Final held in Daejeon, South Korea.

This marked Hanwha Life's first MSI victory and ended Bilibili Gaming's attempt to win every major tournament in a single season, a feat known as the Golden Road. Zeus was named the MSI 2026 Finals MVP. The team competed as the top seed from the LCK during the tournament.

The best-of-five series went the distance, with Hanwha Life Esports winning Game 1. Bilibili Gaming then took strong victories in Games 2 and 3 to lead the series. Hanwha Life Esports responded by winning Games 4 and 5 to secure the championship.

Zeus was a key player throughout the series, using picks like Swain and Dr. Mundo to give Hanwha Life an advantage in the final game. His performance earned him the MVP award for the MSI 2026 Finals.

With the MSI Finals MVP award, Zeus became only the second player in League of Legends history to win both the Worlds Finals MVP and MSI Finals MVP. The first was Faker, a long-time T1 mid laner regarded as one of the greatest League of Legends players ever.

This award highlights a significant rebuild for Hanwha Life. The team had finished last in the 2026 LCK Cup before restructuring around Kanavi and Zeka, who serve as veteran core players, and Zeus and Gumayusi, who joined from T1.

title: Docta Esports vs. ZEN Esports
content:
The competitive League of Legends ecosystem is buzzing following the latest announcement regarding 'Docta Esports vs. ZEN Esports'. As the season progresses, this development has quickly taken center stage, drawing intense scrutiny from fans, analysts, and professional players alike. The core update highlights a significant shift that is expected to have wide-ranging consequences for the regional leagues and international tournaments.

Riot Games continues to refine the competitive structure, balancing the game's high skill ceiling with spectator entertainment. The professional scene is an ever-evolving battleground where even the smallest adjustment can make or break a team's championship aspirations. Every coach, analyst, and player must stay ahead of the curve to remain competitive.

Speaking on the recent events, seasoned esports analysts noted that 'Docta Esports vs. ZEN Esports  Robinhood' is not just a temporary trend but a fundamental shift in how teams will approach their upcoming matchdays. 'Draft priority and map control are going to be completely redefined over the next few weeks,' remarked a prominent league caster. 'Teams that fail to recognize the gravity of this change will find themselves falling behind very quickly in the standings.'

As the community awaits the upcoming weekend matches, anticipation continues to build. Fans can catch all the high-stakes action live on official streaming channels, complete with expert desk analysis and live regional broadcasts. With so much on the line, the next chapter of the 2026 competitive season promises to deliver some of the most memorable moments in League of Legends esports history.
